user_message: I need the team view on QBR preparation. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 10:05 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 13:58 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Nod Publishers

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 15:05 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-03-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:07:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:11:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:21:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:25:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
